title: Education & Experience
text:
Work Experience



Jul 2025 – Ongoing


Language Analyst


 International Centre for Free and Open Source Software (ICFOSS) 



Contributed to the development of a 100+ hour dialectal speech corpus for automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems and carried out socio-phonetic analysis for variation and dialect studies.


Defined phonological, morphological, and syntactic rules for large-scale Malayalam data preparation, including clause boundary detection, anaphora resolution, and discourse connectives.


Processed and curated a 300K-token Malayalam corpus for POS, chunking, NER tagging and ensured quality and consistency for LLM training.


Developed a 170K-word Malayalam lexical dictionary with systematic rule-based validation and quality assurance.





Oct 2023 – Nov 2023


Phonetics Intern


 Living Tongues Institute for Endangered Languages (LTIEL) 



Conducted data annotation and segmentation for Mundari, a Munda language of the Austroasiatic family, using Praat.


Performed detailed acoustic and auditory analyses of speech data to identify key linguistic features.


Supported language documentation efforts by analyzing phonetic and phonological patterns.

ANUGIL VELAYUDHAN


[ˈanuɡil ˈʋeːlaːjud̪ʱan]


Language Analyst | Phonetics | Phonology


 Kerala, India


   






നമസ്കാരം! I am a Language Analyst at the Language Technology and Data Science Lab at the  International Centre for Free and Open Source Software (ICFOSS) , Kerala, India. I am currently engaged in the Discourse-Integrated Dravidian Language to Dravidian Machine Translation Project (DL-Disco MT), which is part of the Bhashini initiative developed by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India. My role involves developing phonological, morphological, and syntactic rules for large-scale Malayalam data preparation, including clause boundary detection, anaphora resolution, and discourse connectives, to develop computational models for Dravidian languages.
